title: Songs Of Shattering III
content:
All the dog-wood blossoms are underneath the tree!
            Ere spring was going--ah, spring is gone!
        And there comes no summer to the like of you and me,--
            Blossom time is early, but no fruit sets on.

        All the dog-wood blossoms are underneath the tree,
            Browned at the edges, turned in a day;
        And I would with all my heart they trimmed a mound for me,
            And weeds were tall on all the paths that led that way!
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Edna St. Vincent Millay delivers a powerful performance in "Songs Of Shattering III"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
